name: Ensure IAM Role can be assumed only by specific Principals
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **iAM Role can be assumed only by specific Principals** across AWS.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on iAM Role can be assumed only by specific Principals. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.
description: IAM Role can be assumed only by specific Principals
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

### Console
1. Open IAM role trust policy.
2. Remove broad principals such as wildcard principal.
3. Allow only explicit trusted principals.
4. Add conditions like sts:ExternalId when third-party access is required.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws iam update-assume-role-policy \
  --role-name <role-name> \
  --policy-document file://trust-policy.json
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_update-role-trust-policy.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/iam/update-assume-role-policy.html
